texto: Like all our memories, we like to take it out once in a while and lay it flat on the kitchen table, the way my wife does with her sewing patterns, where we line up the shape of our life against that which we thought it would be by now.I'll tell you what I don't tell her, that there is something shameful in this, the buoying of our sinking spirits with old stories.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Claire Vay Watkins: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Like all our memories, we like to take it out once in a while and lay it flat on the kitchen tabl…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, comparison, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de love e memories.
